faq.title: Answers to Your Questions
faq.description: Most popular questions about our solutions and services

questions.question: What are the timeframes for greenhouse project implementation?
questions.answer: Timeframes depend on project scale: for small farm greenhouses (up to 1 Ha) — 2-3 months, for industrial complexes (5-15 Ha) — 4-8 months. This includes design, equipment delivery, installation and commissioning.

questions.question: Do you provide equipment warranty?
questions.answer: Yes, we provide warranty on all equipment from 12 to 36 months depending on type. Post-warranty service, spare parts supply and 24/7 tech support are also available.

questions.question: Can you modernize existing greenhouses?
questions.answer: Absolutely! We conduct audits of existing facilities and offer modernization solutions: automation systems installation, covering replacement, drip irrigation implementation, energy-saving screens and other equipment.

questions.question: What is the ROI for greenhouse complex investment?
questions.answer: ROI depends on crop, technology and market. On average, industrial complexes pay back in 2-4 years, farm greenhouses — in 1.5-3 years. We develop detailed business plans with ROI calculations for each project.

questions.question: Do you train staff to operate equipment?
questions.answer: Yes, staff training is included in facility launch services. We conduct theoretical and practical sessions for agronomists, engineers and operators. Technical documentation in Russian is also provided.

questions.question: Do you work throughout the CIS?
questions.answer: Yes, we have offices in Russia, Belarus and partners in Kazakhstan. We've completed projects in 12 CIS countries and provide local support in all regions of presence.

faq.contactPrompt: Our specialists will answer any questions about our solutions

greenhouseStructures.heroDescription: Four product lines for every need: from tunnel and multi-row greenhouses to berry tunnels and utility blocks. Explore 3D models and discover key structural nodes.
greenhouseStructures.exploreHint: Hover over the model nodes to explore details

sections.title: Tunnel Greenhouses
sections.description: Hot-dip galvanized structures 8–18 m wide, 30–150 m long. Double-layer film with air inflation provides superior thermal protection. Wide range of ventilation solutions.
specs: EVA film with 5+ year service life

nodes.label: Roof Arch
nodes.description: EVA film with 5+ year service life. Hot-dip galvanized arches. Double-layer covering with air inflation for superior thermal insulation.

nodes.label: Side Ventilation
nodes.description: Adjustable side vents with anti-wind insect screen. Provides even airflow along the entire length of the greenhouse.

nodes.label: Foundation / Posts
nodes.description: Embedded posts made of hot-dip galvanized steel. Structure designed for 8–18 m span width and up to 150 m length.

nodes.label: Ridge Ventilation
nodes.description: Top ridge vents for natural hot-air convection. Double-layer film with air inflation between layers for thermal efficiency.

nodes.label: End Doors
nodes.description: End doors for machinery access and personnel passage. Various opening mechanisms available.

sections.title: Multi-Row Greenhouses
sections.description: Block structures 9.6 m wide with roof height of 4.7 m or 7.7 m. Connected into a single greenhouse complex via gutters. 8 mm cellular polycarbonate on gable ends.

nodes.label: Block Connecting Gutter
nodes.description: Aluminum gutter connects adjacent blocks into a single complex. Efficiently drains precipitation.

nodes.label: Ridge Ventilation
nodes.description: Single or double-sided ridge ventilation. Ensures thermal convection and removal of humid air.

nodes.label: Side Ventilation
nodes.description: Double-sided side ventilation with insect screen. Ensures even air exchange across the full complex width.

nodes.label: Doors
nodes.description: Type 1: 3.2×2.2 m doors. Type 2: 3.2×3.4 m doors. Designed for small agricultural machinery access.

nodes.label: Polycarbonate End Walls
nodes.description: 8 mm cellular polycarbonate. High light transmittance, excellent thermal insulation, long service life.

sections.title: Berry Tunnels
sections.description: Specialized structures 8–9 m wide with a 5 m ridge height. Galvanized flat-oval tube 80×40 mm. Stainless steel posts Ø51 mm for maximum durability.

nodes.label: Arch Frame
nodes.description: Galvanized flat-oval tube 80×40 mm. Provides high structural strength with minimal weight and shading.

nodes.label: Posts Ø51 mm
nodes.description: Galvanized pipe posts Ø51 mm. Resistant to corrosion, mechanical loads and environmental exposure.

nodes.label: Film Covering
nodes.description: Single-layer film without anti-condensation coating. Optimal solution for growing berry crops.

nodes.label: Film Fastening
nodes.description: 6 mm polyester rope for film attachment. Ensures reliable film tension without damage.

nodes.label: Ridge
nodes.description: 5 m ridge height. Optimal air volume for creating the right microclimate for berry crops.

sections.title: Utility Block
sections.description: Multi-purpose greenhouse annexes 6.4 m or 9.6 m wide. Simultaneously serve as storage, technical room and sales area. Sliding doors for machinery access.

nodes.label: Sliding Doors
nodes.description: 3.2×3.2 m (or 4.5×4.6 m) sliding doors. Provides convenient access for small machinery and loading operations.

nodes.label: Wall Panels
nodes.description: 8 mm cellular polycarbonate around perimeter. Provides natural lighting and good thermal insulation.

nodes.label: Dome Roof
nodes.description: Polyethylene film in 2 layers with air inflation. High thermal insulation properties with low structural weight.

nodes.label: Arch Frame
nodes.description: Flat-oval galvanized steel tube 80×40 mm. High bending strength. Withstands significant snow and wind loads.

engineeringSystems.heroDescription: Comprehensive equipment for optimal microclimate: from ventilation and curtain systems to automation and heating. Everything needed for high yields.
engineeringSystems.comingSoonDescription: Information on shading systems will be added soon. Contact us for current details.

product.name: Circulation Fans
product.description: Mix the air mass of the greenhouse in bi-directional mode, or ventilate the greenhouse space in single-direction mode.
specs: Kit includes: fan, mounting bracket, cabling

sections.title: Shading
sections.introTitle: Greenhouse shading system: plant protection from overheating
sections.intro: Plants suffer from excessive sun: overheating occurs, and leaves, flowers, and fruits can get burned, especially in shade-loving crops. In hot weather, moisture evaporates faster, so shading is useful even for light-loving plants during sustained heat. The system is effective both in greenhouses and in open-field growing.

detailGroups.title: Benefits
items: Temperature reduction by 5–10°C — combined with ventilation and drip misting, it creates an optimal microclimate for crops and rooting cuttings
Breathable structure — the material lets air and water through and does not create a greenhouse effect. Ideal for protection against early-spring burns
Season-long use — a universal solution from February to October for a wide range of tasks

detailGroups.title: Materials and Durability
detailGroups.text: The mesh is made from modern polymers: polypropylene, nylon, and most often high-density polyethylene (HDPE). Knot weave prevents the fabric from unraveling when cut. It is resistant to ultraviolet radiation, precipitation, and frost. Service life is 3–5 seasons with careful use.

detailGroups.title: Shading Percentage
detailGroups.text: This parameter shows how much sunlight the material blocks. The higher the percentage, the less light the plant receives. The range is from 15–20% to 80–90%, selected according to crop requirements.

detailGroups.title: Installation
detailGroups.text: The mesh is attached to the greenhouse frame using ties, clips, and other fastening systems. Installation is quick and does not require complex equipment.

sections.title: Curtain Systems
sections.description: Curtain systems provide energy savings and light regulation. Available in cable and rack configurations for any greenhouse structure.

sections.title: Automation
sections.description: Automated management of all greenhouse systems. We offer budget, mid-range and premium-class solutions depending on the number of control zones.

tiers.name: Budget Level
specs: Weather station: rain, wind speed/direction, illumination, temperature sensors

tiers.name: Mid & Premium Level
specs: External equipment control: irrigation, fans, heating, watering booms
Link up to 10 greenhouses into a complex
Admin console on a dedicated PC

product.name: Heat Generators
product.description: Heat energy source for industrial greenhouses, floor-standing or suspended, various capacities and fuels.
specs: Kit includes: pre-heat filter, deaerator filter, moisture separator, manual control panel, thermostat, cable set

sections.title: Airing
sections.description: Climate control systems ensure, monitor and manage the microclimate in greenhouses. Maintaining key parameters is key to high yields.

products.name: Top Ventilation System
specs: Opening/closing via geared motor drive system
Covering: single/double film or cellular polycarbonate

products.name: Side Ventilation System
specs: Multiple opening types: vertical, side-opening, roll-up

irrigationSystems.heroDescription: Automated irrigation booms for industrial greenhouses and growing fields — precise fertilizer dosing, sectional watering, and multi-function control.
irrigationSystems.controlPanelDescription: Multi-function control unit — various irrigation mode settings, algorithm programming, and system parameter monitoring.

product.name: Irrigation Boom for Industrial Greenhouses (manufactured by Innovatech)
product.description: Watering boom (greenhouse type) — a fully automatic device installed in a greenhouse for watering, fertilizer application, and microclimate regulation. Equipment functionality: organized plant watering on a scheduled basis. Precise liquid fertilizer application. Use of nozzles of varying dispersion. The boom supports phytolamp installation for supplemental plant lighting.
specs: precision fertilizer dosing system «DOSATRON» or equivalent
multi-function control unit with controller and panel

pairs.title: Irrigation Boom for Growing Fields
pairs.description: Equipment functionality: organized watering and liquid fertilizer application on hardening platforms. The built-in control system allows selecting irrigation algorithms. Triple nozzles provide watering, feeding, and fine-mist spraying for plant cooling. Side sprinklers additionally irrigate edge rows exposed to moisture evaporation.
specs: frame material: hot-dip galvanized steel profiles
irrigation boom water consumption: from 3 to 16 m³/h
pressure gauge for water pressure monitoring in the system
sectional boom operation - left and right
multi-function control unit in Russian language

pairs.title: Construction & Control
pairs.description: The trolley with polyurethane guides travels along rails. Rail material: hot-dip galvanized steel. The trolley is driven by an electric gear motor. Watering is performed through nozzles and additional edge sprinklers (side watering). Liquids, fertilizers and chemicals are fed to the nozzles through plastic pipes. The fertilizer dosing device is a special tank with a mechanical dosator. Irrigation is controlled via the control panel. This panel supports various irrigation mode settings.

substratEquipment.heroDescription: Equipment for loosening, careful fluffing and blending substrate with various components: peat, fertilizers, perlite, starch. Suitable for forest and flower nurseries, greenhouse facilities, and seed production centers.

product.name: Big Bale Substrate Fluffer
product.description: Specialized equipment for unpacking and fluffing substrate vacuum-pressed into large bales of 6 m³.
specs: Suitable for flower, vegetable and forest substrates
Designed for unpacking vacuum-pressed substrate with volume of 6 m³
Fluffs substrate without destroying its composition and structure

product.name: Briquette Substrate Fluffers
product.description: Equipment for unpacking and fluffing substrates vacuum-pressed with volume up to 300 L.
specs: Suitable for flower, vegetable and forest substrates
Designed for unpacking vacuum-pressed substrates with volume up to 300 L
Fluffs substrate without destroying its composition and structure

product.name: Substrate Mixer
product.description: Equipment for blending various substrate ingredients with automatic lifting and unloading.
specs: Designed for mixing various substrate ingredients
Capacity up to 2,000 L per cycle
Uniformly mixes without destroying substrate composition and structure

product.name: Substrate Blender
product.description: Professional blender for mixing peat, fertilizers, perlite, starch and other bulk components.
specs: Suitable for flower, vegetable and forest substrates
Mixes peat, fertilizers, perlite, starch and other bulk products
Uniformly mixes without destroying substrate composition and structure

product.name: Substrate Loosener
product.description: Equipment for layer-by-layer loosening of peat substrate with automatic level control in the receiving hopper.
specs: Suitable for flower, vegetable and forest substrates
Maximum weight of processed bale — 1,400 kg
Automatic control of fluffed peat level in the receiving hopper
Scraper chain performs layer-by-layer peat removal and feeding to the fluffing device

lg2400.purpose: The Big Bale fluffer is specifically designed for automatic processing of large compressed bales. Peat is restored to its original structure and volume. Suitable for Big Bales up to 1200×1200×2400 mm. The fluffer has proven its versatility in practice and requires modest investment with rapid return.
features: All protruding or rotating machine parts are maximally safe for the operator
Machine is fully PLC-controlled with all functions displayed on screen
Variable frequency drive lift control provides medium speed pulling, minimum processing speed and fast return
High homogeneity and uniformity of processed soil through combined vertical and horizontal operation
No peat collapse due to scraper effect during horizontal mowing
Bale secured inside the machine retains required moisture level during idle periods
Optical sensor for automatic load level monitoring

peatFilling.heroDescription: Automated machines and lines for filling seedling pots, trays and containers with peat substrate. Mosa Green and Sedlin equipment delivers even fill density and precise hole drilling for subsequent planting.

sections.title: Mosa Green Pot Filling Machine
sections.description: An electro-pneumatic machine for filling plastic pots, both round and square, complete with a single-column pot dispenser featuring a rotating head for 4 operations: auger (including a full set of 7 bits of different diameters), ejector arm and transfer conveyor. Machine speed and substrate loading can be adjusted to achieve the desired fill density. Quick and simple setup of the various devices allows fast pot-size changes and a wide range of diameters. Suitable for pots from 8 to 22 cm, with a 1-column pot dispenser, drilling station and a 3 m fixed-speed conveyor.

sections.title: Mosa Green Tray Filler
sections.description: A machine for evenly filling seedling trays with peat substrate, featuring a dual compaction system and control over substrate concentration in the trays.
features: Special filler design with fully adjustable speed for controlling substrate concentration in the trays
Dual compaction system: vibrating table under the tray, compaction propeller above the tray
Excess substrate return system back to the hopper
Brush for cleaning the filled tray

sections.title: Sedlin Large Pot Filling Machine
sections.description: Designed for filling large-volume seedling pots with substrate (peat) and drilling holes for subsequent planting.

sections.title: Sedlin Pot Filling Machine
sections.description: Designed for filling seedling pots with substrate (peat) and drilling holes for subsequent planting.

sections.title: P9 Sedlin Tray and Pot Filler
sections.description: Designed for filling seedling trays and small seedling pots with substrate (peat). Can be used both as part of a line and as a stand-alone unit.

cultivationEquipment.heroDescription: Cultivation benches and hydroponic insert trays with an ebb-and-flow (flood-and-drain) system for growing flowers, ornamental plants and seedlings in nurseries, garden centres and greenhouses.

sections.title: Cultivation units (benches)
sections.description: Cultivation units (benches) are designed for placing and tending plants: growing flowers and ornamental plants in nurseries, holding pots with seedlings in garden centres, and organising seedling and lettuce sections in greenhouses.

principle.label: How it works
principle.intro: Plants in pots or trays are placed on the tabletop. Watering is carried out through an ebb-and-flow (flood-and-drain) system.

steps.title: Flood
steps.text: Water is supplied into the tray through valves, filling it to a set level with a pump.

steps.title: Drain
steps.text: Water drains away through a valve and hose into the drainage system.

variants: Rolling bench with a plastic hydroponic tray and ebb-and-flow system
Stationary bench with a plastic hydroponic tray and ebb-and-flow system
Rolling bench with a galvanised grid (mesh)
Stationary bench with a galvanised grid (mesh)

specs.value: 3 mm ABS plastic with UV protection

sections.title: Insert trays for cultivation units
sections.description: Insert trays are designed to be placed on top of cultivation benches as a hydroponic tray with an ebb-and-flow system. They hold pots and trays, supply and drain water, protect the bench from moisture, and distribute moisture evenly to the roots.
features: Longitudinal and transverse channels for even water distribution
Drain channels for complete water removal
Standard 3 mm thickness — service life of at least 5 years
Reinforced 4 mm version — service life of up to 10 years

paperPot.heroDescription: The complete INNOVATECH equipment range for making biodegradable paper pots (plugs), filling them with substrate, sowing seeds and arranging them into trays — from compact machines to fully automated lines.

benefits.title: Advantages of paper pots
benefits.subtitle: PaperPot technology means healthy seedlings, even emergence and transplanting without stress. The paper pot goes into the ground together with the plant and fully decomposes, leaving no plastic behind.

items.title: 100% biodegradable
items.text: The pot fully decomposes in the soil within 3–6 months. Plant straight into the ground — no plastic trays, disposal or waste.

items.title: Transplanting without stress
items.text: The plant is set out together with its pot, so the root system stays intact. Survival rate is close to 100%, with no seedling losses.

items.title: Healthy root system
items.text: Air root pruning prevents roots from spiralling and builds a strong, fibrous root system before the plant is ever transplanted.

items.title: Earlier, even harvest
items.text: Seedlings start growing right after planting and develop uniformly — reaching marketable produce sooner.

items.title: Labour savings
items.text: Automatic forming, substrate filling and planting together with the pot speed up planting work several times over.

items.title: Eco-friendly image
items.text: Eliminating plastic cuts logistics and disposal costs and underlines the sustainability of your production.

sections.title: Single-channel paper pot making machine (AGIB)
sections.description: The INNOVATECH single-channel machine is designed to make paper pots (plugs) for seedlings of flowers, berries, fruit trees and forest seedlings, filling them with soil or substrate at the same time. It automatically forms a pot of the required size and fills it immediately. Suitable for use with both special paper (85% cotton, 15% polyester) and nonwoven material.

principle.label: Operating principle
principle.text: The machine automatically makes paper seedling pots from roll material while filling them with substrate at the same time. It forms pots of the set diameter and length, fills them evenly with substrate and feeds the finished products onto a conveyor or into a pot tray loader (optional).

features: can be used with a pot arranging unit

specs.value: 4.8 kW / 3×400 V AC + N + PE, 50 Hz

sections.title: Dual-channel paper pot making machine (AGIB202)
sections.description: The INNOVATECH dual-channel machine is designed for automatic making and filling of paper pots from biodegradable paper (85% cotton, 15% polyester) for growing seedlings of flowers, berries, fruit trees and forest seedlings. The equipment delivers high capacity and uniform pot formation, and works with both paper and nonwoven material. The package includes a sorting conveyor that allows paper pots to be packed into trays by hand.

principle.label: Operating principle
principle.text: The machine automatically makes paper seedling pots from roll material and then fills them with substrate. It forms pots of the set diameter and length, fills them evenly with peat and feeds the finished products to the arranging or sowing line. The dual-channel system delivers high capacity and the ability to work with different pot diameters at the same time.

features: can be used with a pot arranging unit

specs.value: 4.55 kW / 3×400 V AC + N + PE, 50 Hz

sections.title: Paper pot tray loading machine (ATI)
sections.description: The machine is designed for the automatic loading of finished paper seedling pots into trays. It can operate either as a standalone unit or as part of a line.

principle.label: Operating principle
principle.text: Finished paper pots (plugs) are loaded into the machine; the device positions each pot and places them into the tray cells. The machine counts the paper pots and has a convenient, simple interface.

sections.title: Paper pot tray loader (ATIB)
sections.description: The machine is designed for the automated loading of paper pots (plugs) into trays. It is an upgraded version of the ATI with higher capacity and compatibility with equipment for automatic tray feeding and removal.

principle.label: Operating principle
principle.text: Finished paper pots filled with substrate are loaded into the machine, which then automatically places them into trays.

features: adjustable parameters for different pot sizes and soil types

specs.value: 2 kW / 220 V AC + N + PE, 50 Hz

sections.title: Dual-channel paper pot loader (ATIB TURBO)
sections.description: The ATIB TURBO is the dual-channel version of the ATIB, designed to fill two rows of paper pots with substrate at the same time and then arrange them into trays.

principle.label: Operating principle
principle.text: Finished paper pots are loaded into the machine's two parallel channels. Both channels run in sync and the finished pots are placed into trays. A dibbler can then be added (optional) and the pots moved onto a conveyor.

features: compatible with automatic tray feeding and removal equipment

sections.title: Automated line for making and sowing into paper pots (PAL)
sections.description: The line is designed for highly precise automated sowing of seeds into paper pots. Its key difference from the multi-channel sowing line is lower capacity and the fact that this line is not equipped with automatic tray feeding and removal.

principle.label: Operating principle
principle.text: The line makes paper pots (plugs). After filling with substrate and pre-watering with dibbling, seeds are sown into the pots (the line self-adjusts depth and spacing for different seed types). After additional mulching and watering, the trays of pots are fed onto the final roller table.

nodes: AGIB single-channel paper pot making machine

specs.value: 7.3 kW / 3×400 V AC + N + PE, 50 Hz

sections.title: Automated line for sowing into paper pots (PALB)
sections.description: PALB is a fully automatic line for making paper pots (plugs) and sowing seeds into them, performing the following functions: filling with substrate, arranging the finished pots into trays, dibbling, precise seed sowing, mulching and moistening. PALB differs from PAL in its high capacity and compatibility with automatic tray feeding and removal.

principle.label: Operating principle
principle.text: The line makes and loads finished paper pots (plugs), after which each pot is placed into trays. Then, after pre-moistening and dibbling, precise spot sowing takes place (the line self-adjusts depth and spacing for different seed types). The sown trays of pots are additionally mulched and moistened and fed onto the final conveyor.

nodes: AGIB single-channel paper pot making machine

specs.value: 8 kW / 3×400 V AC + N + PE, 50 Hz

sections.title: Dual-channel line for sowing into paper pots (PALB TURBO)
sections.description: PALB TURBO is a dual-channel, fully automatic line for making and sowing seeds into paper pots, performing the following functions: making paper pots (plugs), arranging the finished pots into trays, dibbling, precise seed sowing, mulching and moistening. PALB TURBO differs from PALB in its doubled capacity thanks to two channels working in parallel.

principle.label: Operating principle
principle.text: The line makes and loads finished paper pots (plugs), after which each pot is placed into trays. Then, after pre-moistening and dibbling, precise spot sowing takes place (the line self-adjusts depth and spacing for different seed types). The sown trays of pots are additionally mulched and moistened and fed onto the final conveyor.

nodes: AGIB202 dual-channel paper pot making machine
features: two parallel channels for filling and sowing

specs.value: 8.4 kW / 3×400 V AC + N + PE, 50 Hz

sections.title: Multi-channel automated line for sowing into paper pots (MAE)
sections.description: The line is designed for making paper pots (plugs) and fully automated sowing of seeds into them. It performs the following operations: multi-channel making and filling of pots with substrate, precise seed sowing, and arranging the finished pots into trays. The line is fitted with IoT sensors, enabling control and monitoring from any device.

principle.label: Operating principle
principle.text: The line makes paper pots (plugs) using a multi-channel machine. A substrate feeding unit (peat fluffer) is connected to it. The pots filled with substrate are automatically arranged into trays. Seeds are sown into the pots at a set depth and spacing using a drum seeder. The trays of pots are then additionally mulched, watered and discharged onto the final conveyor. Empty trays are fed automatically and filled trays are removed automatically.

nodes: Paper pot filling and tray loading unit

specs.value: 25.5 kW / 3×400 V AC + N + PE, 50 Hz

sections.title: Paper for making paper pots (plugs)
sections.description: The paper is designed for making paper pots (plugs) for seedlings. It has an adhesive layer on one side. It is used for growing flowers, berries, vegetables, forest seedlings and fruit crops. Paper pots have high water-holding capacity, create a favorable environment for root system development and hold their shape throughout the entire seedling stage. The paper fully decomposes in 3–6 months.

principle.label: Operating principle
principle.text: The paper is loaded into the paper pot making machine, which then forms a sleeve of the set diameter and length while filling it with peat (substrate). The sleeve is then cut into pots (plugs) of the set size. They are suitable for sowing seeds or rooting cuttings, either after transplanting into trays or on their own. Once the seedlings reach the required size, the plant is set into the ground together with the pot. Over 3–6 months the paper decomposes in the soil, with no removal required.

features: rolls of various widths, 1400 m long

sections.title: Mini-line 1 (AGIB + ATIB)
sections.description: The line is designed for the fully automated production of paper pots (plugs) filled with substrate, dibbling, and arranging the finished pots into trays with discharge onto a distribution roller table. Available in two configurations — with or without a peat fluffer.
configs: INNOVATECH AGIB + ATIB — single-channel paper pot making (AGIB), filling the pots with substrate (ATIB), creating a planting hole in each pot, automatic tray feeding, touchscreen, final roller table.

INNOVATECH AGIB + ATIB + PB600 — automatic feeding of fluffed peat (PB600), single-channel paper pot making (AGIB), single-channel filling with substrate (ATIB), creating a planting hole in each pot, automatic tray feeding, touchscreen, final roller table.
features: single-channel making and filling of pots

sections.title: Mini-line 2 (AGIB202 + ATIB TURBO)
sections.description: The line is designed for the fully automated production of paper pots (plugs) filled with substrate, dibbling, and arranging the finished pots into trays with discharge onto a distribution roller table. Available in two configurations — with or without a peat fluffer.
configs: INNOVATECH AGIB202 + ATIB TURBO — dual-channel paper pot making (AGIB202), dual-channel filling of the pots with substrate (ATIB TURBO), creating a planting hole in each pot, automatic tray feeding, touchscreen, final roller table.

INNOVATECH AGIB202 + ATIB TURBO + PB600 — INNOVATECH peat fluffer (PB600), dual-channel paper pot making (AGIB202), dual-channel filling with substrate (ATIB TURBO), creating a planting hole in each pot, automatic tray feeding, touchscreen, final roller table.
features: doubled capacity thanks to two channels
dual-channel making and filling of pots
